channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Paramount CEO David Ellison Assures '60 Minutes' Correspondent of Editorial Independence Amid Recent CBS News Staff Changes
item.description: Paramount CEO David Ellison recently contacted '60 Minutes' correspondent Lesley Stahl to assure her that the iconic newsmagazine program would maintain its editorial independence. This statement come...

item.title: Sen. Thom Tillis Criticizes Senate Republicans Over Internal Divisions and Support for Controversial Trump Programs
item.description: Retiring Senator Thom Tillis of North Carolina has sparked debate within the Republican Senate caucus after sending a lengthy 15-page letter criticizing fellow Republicans over their handling of key l...

item.title: Jasmine Crockett Challenges Alveda King's Interpretation of Martin Luther King Jr.'s Legacy During House Hearing
item.description: A contentious exchange unfolded during a House Judiciary Committee hearing focused on the Southern Poverty Law Center (SPLC), where conservative activist Alveda King appeared as a Republican witness. ...

item.title: Report: U.S. officials privately concerned Iran is rebuilding military capacity during ceasefire
item.description: According to a CNN report cited by Raw Story, unnamed Trump administration officials have expressed private concern that a ceasefire between the United States and Iran may be allowing Tehran time to r...

item.title: GOP Witness Alveda King Interrupts House Judiciary Hearing on SPLC with Controversial Remarks on Abortion and Infant Genitalia
item.description: During a House Judiciary Committee hearing titled 'The Southern Poverty Law Center: Manufacturing Hate, Part II,' a GOP witness, Dr. Alveda King, made an unexpected and inflammatory statement while av...

item.title: Unequal treatment of Black South African soldiers after World War II
item.description: The article examines the stark racial inequalities faced by Black South African soldiers who served during World War II. Around 77,000 Black South Africans volunteered or were recruited into the Nativ...

item.title: Report Examines Historical Ties Between Bill Pulte’s Family and the Fellowship Foundation
item.description: A report by journalist Jonathan Larsen examines longstanding connections between the family of Bill Pulte, the acting U.S. national intelligence director, and a religious organization known as The Fam...

item.title: Netflix Shares First Look at Live-Action 'Scooby-Doo: Origins' Series Filming in Atlanta for 2027 Premiere
item.description: Netflix has released an early first-look clip from its upcoming live-action series 'Scooby-Doo: Origins,' which is scheduled to premiere in 2027. The footage shows the iconic Great Dane running throug...

item.title: Nancy Mace Campaign Alleges Assault by Pamela Evette Finance Team Member Ahead of South Carolina GOP Primary
item.description: A political dispute emerged in South Carolina's Republican gubernatorial primary after the campaign team of Representative Nancy Mace accused a member of Lieutenant Governor Pamela Evette's finance co...

item.title: Morning Joe Panelists Criticize Trump's Election Fraud Claims in Los Angeles Mayoral Race
item.description: On the June 9, 2026 episode of MSNBC's 'Morning Joe,' panelists expressed strong concern over President Donald Trump's recent claims that Republican candidates, including former reality TV star Spence...

item.title: A Historical Look at Cryptography, Espionage Techniques, and Modern Online Privacy
item.description: This article explores the long history of protecting sensitive information and preventing others from accessing private communications. It begins with ancient Rome and the famous Caesar cipher, attrib...

item.title: Paramount Considers Expanding Bari Weiss' Editorial Role Across CBS News and CNN After Merger
item.description: Paramount has reportedly begun preliminary discussions with several senior media executives as it explores creating a new business leadership position that could complement the role of CBS News editor...

item.title: Vintage Photos Celebrate Lizabeth Scott’s Enduring Legacy as a Film Noir Star
item.description: This article highlights the life, career, and lasting appeal of American actress Lizabeth Scott (1922–2015), one of the most recognizable faces of classic film noir during the 1940s and 1950s. Known f...

item.title: Opinion Writer Criticizes White House Page Targeting Three Pro-Trump Critics
item.description: This opinion article by Tara Dublin argues that a recently published White House webpage identifying three individuals as 'Leftist Influencers' represents a troubling escalation in the Trump administr...

item.title: Rare Behind-the-Scenes Photos of Michael J. Fox During the Filming of Doc Hollywood and the Onset of Parkinson’s Symptoms
item.description: The article revisits the production of the 1991 romantic comedy “Doc Hollywood,” starring Michael J. Fox, and explains why the film occupies a significant place in the actor’s personal history. While ...

item.title: President Zardari urges focus on public welfare, provincial rights and economic stability ahead of FY2026-27 budget
item.description: President Asif Ali Zardari met Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif at the President House to discuss the upcoming federal budget for FY2026-27, which is scheduled to be presented on June 10. During the meet...

item.title: Rachel Maddow Highlights Negative Crowd Reaction to Trump at NBA Finals Game in New York
item.description: MSNBC host Rachel Maddow devoted part of her program to President Donald Trump's appearance at Game 3 of the NBA Finals at Madison Square Garden in New York City. During the broadcast, Maddow showed f...

item.description: Election data analyst Harry Enten appeared on CNN to address conspiracy theories spread by President Donald Trump's supporters following the elimination of MAGA-aligned reality star Spencer Pratt from...

item.title: National Security Critics Question Trump's Appointment of Bill Pulte as Director of National Intelligence

item.title: Color Photographs Document Daily Life, Growth, and Tourism in 1950s Florida
item.description: This article presents a collection of color photographs from Florida during the 1950s, illustrating a period of significant demographic growth, economic development, and social change across the state...
